title: calophyllum macrocarpum
intro: Calophyllum macrocarpum, also known as the Kamani tree, is a tropical plant species native to the Pacific Islands, Australia, and Southeast Asia. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its seed oil, which is rich in fatty acids and has been used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections, wounds, and inflammation. The oil has been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, providing several health benefits, such as promoting wound healing, reducing inflammation, and protecting against infections. Herbal preparations of the Kamani tree, including oil extracts and topical creams, are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat skin conditions and promote overall well-being.
